DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT RUIPING WU'S MOTION TO HAVE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS DEEMED ADMITTED. NO APPEARANCES. THOSE WHO HAVE NOT MADE AN APPEARANCE ARE DEEMED TO HAVE STIPULATED TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE MOTION WAS UNOPPOSED. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS SET ONE, REQUESTS NOS. 1 AND 2, ARE HEREBY DEEMED ADMITTED. THE COURT ALSO GRANTS THE REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF IN THE AMOUNT OF $310.00. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE PRO TEM: DAVID HELBRAUN, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT RUIPING WU'S MOTION TO HAVE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS DEEMED ADMITTED.

No appearances. Those who have not made an appearance are deemed to have stipulated to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: the motion was unopposed. Defendant's Request for Admissions Set One, Requests Nos. 1 and 2, are hereby deemed admitted. The Court also grants the request for sanctions against plaintiff in the amount of $310.00. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge Pro Tem: David Helbraun, Clerk: E.
